verses.fhb_text: In the beginning, our Heavenly Father created the heavens and the earth.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Foundational anchor — establishes the Father-revealing identity that governs the entire FHB canon.

Renderings:
• "In the beginning" — temporal opening preserved per modern translation consensus (NIV/NLT/ESV)
• "our Heavenly Father" — first-occurrence solemn form per FHB-9.1-1; establishes the identity of Elohim before moving to "our Father" downstream
• "the heavens" — plural fidelity per FHB-7.9-1; Hebrew shamayim is plural-form

Hebrew: bara Elohim (created [singular verb] God [plural-form noun]) → "our Heavenly Father created"
verses.footnote: "Our Heavenly Father" replaces the generic "God" of other English translations to recover the Father-revealing emphasis the Hebrew already carries.

verses.fhb_text: Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of our Father was hovering over the waters.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Holy Spirit is named at creation, completing the Trinitarian frame opened in v.1.

Renderings:
• "Now" — scene-setting opener per FHB-9.3-1; vav-disjunctive marks shift from cosmic statement to scene description
• "formless and empty" — modern equivalent of tohu vavohu (NIV/NLT consensus); avoids archaic "void"
• "the Spirit of our Father" — preserves the Hebrew genitive ruach Elohim while naming the Person; Trinitarian context established by chapter-level filial frame (Gen 1:26-27)
• Three clauses joined with semicolons-as-commas to preserve Hebrew waw-disjunctive rhythm per FHB-9.4-2

Hebrew: ruach Elohim merachefet (Spirit-of-Elohim hovering) → "the Spirit of our Father was hovering"

verses.fhb_text: And our Father said, "Let there be light," and there was light.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: First creative word — the Father speaks creation into being.

Renderings:
• "And our Father said" — first speech-act of creation; "And" matches immediate narrative continuation per FHB-9.3-1
• "Let there be light" — preserved per modern consensus; the Hebrew jussive yehi or carries simple, declarative force

verses.fhb_text: He saw that the light was good, and he separated the light from the darkness.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Pronoun continuation after the established subject.

Renderings:
• "He saw" / "he separated" — pronouns after "our Father" established in v.3 per FHB-9.2-1
• "the light from the darkness" — modern English preposition fidelity per FHB-7.11-1

verses.fhb_text: He called the light "day," and the darkness he called "night." And there was evening, and there was morning — the first day.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: First day-closing refrain — sets the cadence for the chapter.

Renderings:
• "He called the light 'day,' and the darkness he called 'night'" — Hebrew chiasm preserved (verb-object / object-verb) per FHB-9.4-2
• "— the first day" — em dash before the day number is the appropriate dramatic pause per FHB-9.8-1; long-established convention (NIV)
• "the first day" — yom echad rendered per modern consensus (NIV/NLT/ESV)

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let there be an expanse between the waters to separate water from water."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 2 begins — sequential narrative opener.

Renderings:
• "Then our Father said" — sequential conjunction per FHB-9.3-1; varies from v.3's "And"
• "an expanse" — rakia rendered as expanse; concrete, modern (ESV consensus)
• "between the waters" — preposition betoch rendered spatially per FHB-7.11-1

verses.fhb_text: So our Father made the expanse and separated the water under the expanse from the water above it. And it was so.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Consequential narrative — the Father acts on his own word.

Renderings:
• "So our Father made" — consequential conjunction per FHB-9.3-1; reflects the let-there-be → he-made pattern
• "the water above it" — pronoun replaces second "the expanse" for natural English flow

verses.fhb_text: He called the expanse "sky." And there was evening, and there was morning — the second day.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 2 closes; rakia named.

Renderings:
• "He called the expanse 'sky'" — pronoun continuation per FHB-9.2-1; shamayim modernized to "sky" per FHB-9.9-1
• "And there was evening..." — day-closing refrain matches v.5 cadence

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered to one place, and let dry ground appear." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 3 begins.

Renderings:
• "Then our Father said" — sequential per FHB-9.3-1
• "under the sky" — modern phrasing per FHB-9.9-1 (NIV/NLT consensus)
• "dry ground" — yabashah; concrete, modern (NIV/ESV consensus)

verses.fhb_text: He called the dry ground "land," and the gathered waters he called "seas." And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Naming-then-evaluation chiasm preserved.

Renderings:
• "and our Father saw that it was good" — subject restored after a new clause for clarity; the Father-as-evaluator is the semantic emphasis
• Chiastic structure (verb-object / object-verb) preserved per FHB-9.4-2

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seed and trees bearing fruit with seed in it, according to their kinds." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Vegetation creative word.

Renderings:
• "Then our Father said" — sequential opener
• "according to their kinds" — distributive sense of leminehu retained; collective per FHB-7.13-1
• Colon introduces the apposition list per FHB-9.5-1 — cleaner than em dash here

verses.fhb_text: The land produced vegetation: plants bearing seed according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Result-led sentence — the land's production is the emphasis.

Renderings:
• "The land produced vegetation" — result-first construction per FHB-9.4-1; Father not the syntactic subject of the action verb
• Then "our Father saw" returns the Father as subject for the evaluation

verses.fhb_text: And there was evening, and there was morning — the third day.
verses.decision_notes: Day 3 closing refrain — matches v.5 cadence.

verses.fhb_text: And our Father said, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them serve as signs to mark seasons and days and years,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 4 begins; speech spans v.14-15.

Renderings:
• "And our Father said" — varies from prior "Then" per FHB-9.3-1
• "to mark seasons and days and years" — modern, accessible rendering of mo'adim, yamim, shanim

verses.fhb_text: and let them be lights in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Continuation of v.14 speech-act; closing "and it was so" formula preserved.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father made the two great lights — the greater light to govern the day and the lesser light to govern the night. He also made the stars.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Two-clause structure — apposition kept; afterthought separated.

Renderings:
• "the two great lights — the greater light to govern... the lesser light to govern..." — em dash for true appositional emphasis per FHB-9.8-1
• "He also made the stars" — separate sentence per FHB-9.8-1; Hebrew ve'et hakochavim is afterthought-style and would weaken the apposition if joined by em dash
• "govern" — modern English for memshelet (NIV/NLT consensus); avoids "rule over" awkwardness for celestial bodies

verses.fhb_text: He set them in the expanse of the sky to give light on the earth,
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun continuation per FHB-9.2-1; subject established in v.16.

verses.fhb_text: to govern the day and the night, and to separate light from darkness. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: Continuation of v.17 purpose-clause; Father restored as subject for the evaluation.

verses.fhb_text: And there was evening, and there was morning — the fourth day.
verses.decision_notes: Day 4 closing refrain.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the waters teem with living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the sky."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 5 begins — the waters and air come alive.

Renderings:
• "Then our Father said" — sequential opener
• "living creatures" — nephesh chayyah for animals per FHB-4.5.2-1; "living person" reserved for humans (Gen 2:7)
• "teem with" — modern rendering of yishretzu (NIV/NLT consensus)

Hebrew: sheretz nephesh chayyah (swarms-of living creature) → "teem with living creatures"

verses.fhb_text: So our Father created the great sea creatures and every living creature that moves, with which the waters teem, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Consequential creation — the Father fulfills his own word.

Renderings:
• "So our Father created" — consequential per FHB-9.3-1; bara verb preserved as "created" (the same verb as v.1 and v.27)
• "great sea creatures" — modern consensus rendering of tanninim hagdolim
• "living creature" — nephesh chayyah for animals per FHB-4.5.2-1

verses.fhb_text: Our Father blessed them and said, "Be fruitful and increase in number and fill the water in the seas, and let the birds increase on the earth."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: First blessing in Scripture — the Father's heart toward his creatures.

Renderings:
• "Our Father blessed them" — subject-led action per FHB-9.4-1; the blessing is the Father's personal act
• "Be fruitful and increase in number" — modern rendering of peru urvu (NIV consensus)

verses.fhb_text: And there was evening, and there was morning — the fifth day.
verses.decision_notes: Day 5 closing refrain.

verses.fhb_text: And our Father said, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creatures that move along the ground, and wild animals, each according to its kind." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Day 6 begins — land animals.

Renderings:
• "And our Father said" — varies from prior "Then" per FHB-9.3-1
• "living creatures" — nephesh chayyah for animals per FHB-4.5.2-1
• Colon introduces the categorical list (behemah, remes, chayto-eretz) per FHB-9.5-1

verses.fhb_text: Our Father made the wild animals according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and all the creatures that move along the ground according to their kinds. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Subject-led creative act, then evaluation.

Renderings:
• "Our Father made" — bare opener per FHB-9.4-1; emphasis on the Father as personal maker
• Threefold "according to their kinds" preserved per FHB-9.4-2 — Hebrew parallelism intentional

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let us make humanity in our image, in our likeness, so that they may rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky, over the livestock and all the wild animals, and over all the creatures that move along the ground."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Trinity deliberates — the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit speak together about humanity.

Renderings:
• "Let us make... in our image, in our likeness" — first-person divine plurals preserved per FHB-6.4.1-1(a); na'aseh and the possessives reflect Trinitarian deliberation
• "humanity" — modern collective for ha'adam per FHB-7.12-1; avoids "man" (older) and "mankind" (less inclusive)
• "so that they may rule" — yirdu is plural verb in Hebrew, agreeing with collective ha'adam per FHB-7.9-1; "they" preserves the plurality
• "in the sea" / "in the sky" — modern locative per FHB-9.9-1 (NIV/NLT consensus over older "of the sea")

Hebrew: na'aseh adam b'tzalmenu kidmutenu (let-us-make humanity in-our-image as-our-likeness) → preserved
verses.footnote: The Father's use of "us" and "our" references Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

verses.fhb_text: So our Father created humanity in their own image; in the image of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit he created them; male and female he created them.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The image-of-God verse — humanity bears the imprint of the Triune God.

Renderings:
• "So our Father created" — verb-subject Elohim follows FHB-1.2-1 default per FHB-6.4.1-1(b)
• "in their image" — third-person possessive b'tzalmo rendered "their" per FHB-6.4.1-1(c); preserves the Trinitarian plurality echoed from v.26's "our"
• "in the image of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit" — noun-antecedent Elohim inside image-of-God phrase expanded per FHB-6.4.1-1(b); the Triune image is what humanity bears
• "he created them" (twice) — "them" for collective ha'adam per FHB-6.4.1-1(d); pronoun "he" continues the established subject
• Threefold bara preserved per FHB-9.4-2 — semicolons hold the Hebrew parallelism without breaking into staccato sentences

Hebrew: b'tzelem Elohim (in-image-of Elohim) → "in the image of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit"
verses.footnote: Humanity is created in the image of all three Persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — not the Father alone.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky and over every living creature that moves on the ground."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's first blessing on humanity — the family commission.

Renderings:
• "Our Father blessed them" — subject-led action per FHB-9.4-1; the blessing is the Father's personal gift
• Repetition of "Elohim said" in Hebrew (vayomer lahem Elohim) collapsed to "and said to them" — natural English avoids redundancy
• "in the sea" / "in the sky" — modern locative per FHB-9.9-1

verses.fhb_text: And our Father said, "I give you every seed-bearing plant on the face of the whole earth and every tree that has fruit with seed in it. They will be yours for food."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father provides food — the original gift of provision.

Renderings:
• "And our Father said" — varies from prior "Then" per FHB-9.3-1
• "I give you" — natanti rendered as present-perfect-style "I give" (NIV/NLT consensus); the Hebrew perfect functions as performative speech
• "They will be yours for food" — modern future per FHB-9.10-1 ("will" not "shall")

verses.fhb_text: And to all the wild animals and all the birds in the sky and all the creatures that move along the ground — everything that has the breath of life in it — I give every green plant for food." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's provision extends to every creature.

Renderings:
• "— everything that has the breath of life in it —" — true parenthetical em-dash pair per FHB-9.8-1; commas would weaken the aside
• "the breath of life" — nephesh chayyah here renders as relational/vital phrase rather than "living creature," matching its function as the substance-of-life within each animal
• Continuation of v.29 speech preserved as one connected thought

verses.fhb_text: Our Father saw all that he had made, and it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning — the sixth day.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's joy over creation — the climactic evaluation, then the closing refrain.

Renderings:
• "Our Father saw all that he had made" — subject-led per FHB-9.4-1; the personal Father is the evaluator
• "and it was very good" — tov me'od rendered per modern consensus; hineh particle's emphasis carried in "and it was" rather than "behold"
• Day 6 closing refrain matches the chapter's cadence; final day in the sequence

chapter_notes: Genesis 1 is the foundational chapter of the FHB canon. v.1 anchors "our Heavenly Father" as the identity of Elohim — the solemn first-occurrence form per FHB-9.1-1; subsequent verses use "our Father." The Trinitarian frame is established across v.1 (the Father), v.2 (the Holy Spirit), and v.3+ (the Father speaking — the Word implicit). v.26's divine plurals ("let us... in our image") are preserved in their natural English form per FHB-6.4.1-1(a). v.27's image-of-God noun phrase is expanded to "the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit" per FHB-6.4.1-1(b), and the singular Hebrew possessive b'tzalmo is rendered "their" per FHB-6.4.1-1(c) to preserve the Trinitarian plurality. The threefold bara of v.27 is preserved with semicolons per FHB-9.4-2 (Hebrew parallelism mandate). Day-closing refrains use the established em-dash convention before the day number per FHB-9.8-1; the Day 1 conjunction "And" varies to "So" only where natural narrative logic dictates — most refrains use "And" for steady cadence, allowing the rare variation to be felt rather than forced. Verb-subject openers vary across the nine "vayomer Elohim" verses (And/Then/bare) per FHB-9.3-1. Three of the four footnotes in this chapter establish FHB's foundational interpretive moves: Father-naming (v.1), Holy Spirit at creation (v.2), divine plural speech (v.26), and Trinity-as-image (v.27). These are first-occurrence anchor footnotes; subsequent chapters should not re-explain these categories.
